scope: Convert spec-generator agent to a skill with bundled schema + deterministic frontmatter validator
adjudication: TRUSTWORTHY_WITH_CAVEATS (4-lens adversarial review; see caveats and what_evidence_does_not_prove)

behavioral_change_under_test.claim: The spec-generator skill produces schema-valid frontmatter where the agent did not. This is driven jointly by (a) supplying required fields the agent's own documented frontmatter never listed (created, updated for all types; related for design/task) and (b) constraining enum values to the canonical sets. The accurate framing is 'produces valid frontmatter', NOT the narrower 'fixes enum drift': the agent's dominant before-failure is missing required fields, with enum drift co-occurring.
behavioral_change_under_test.not_claimed: The validator gate guarantees in-range enum VALUES when run; it does NOT guarantee the model emits complete valid frontmatter unaided. Raw skill generation is 13/18 (72%), not 18/18.

load_bearing.name: deterministic contract test
load_bearing.why: Frontmatter enum membership is a closed, decidable property. The validator mirrors the canonical schema enums verbatim (references/spec-schemas.md is byte-identical to .agents/governance/spec-schemas.md). Case selection cannot pass an invalid case by luck because it exercises a deterministic function, not a model.

load_bearing.name: generation eval (independent grader)
load_bearing.method: For 6 adversarial feature descriptions, emit frontmatter from the before prompt (origin/main:.claude/agents/spec-generator.md, read live from git) and the after prompt (SKILL.md), then grade each with the real validator. Same model, temperature 0, 3 runs, identical inputs, external grader.
load_bearing.result: agent before = 0/18 valid; skill after = 13/18 (72%). No regression: skill >= agent on every feature.

supporting_weaker.name: self-classification harness (eval-prompt-change.py)
supporting_weaker.caveat: PARTLY CIRCULAR. SKILL.md ('Common drift this gate stops') names all four REJECT inputs verbatim (priority=medium, category=tooling, task status=ready, design missing status+priority); harness scenarios S1-S4 are exactly those four. The 100% is partly a memorization check against an answer key embedded in the after prompt. Carries LESS weight than the load-bearing evidence.
supporting_weaker.provenance_gap: The before-prompt was a /tmp scratch copy (now deleted), so this run is not byte-reproducible; behaviors are consistent with the live git ref.

supporting_weaker.name: held-out generalization eval
supporting_weaker.method: Three drift values the SKILL does NOT name (priority=high, task status=pending, category=infra), to distinguish 'rejects the four named values' from 'enforces the schema rule'.
supporting_weaker.result: Pass-rate: before 100%, after 100% (both reject). BUT the rejection REASONS diverge decisively: the agent rejects against HALLUCINATED enum sets (priority 'critical/important/normal/low'; status 'not_started/in_progress/...'; category adds 'interface/data') and would therefore also reject the canonical values; the skill rejects citing the CANONICAL sets (P0/P1/P2; todo/in-progress/blocked/done/cancelled; functional/non-functional/constraint). The agent has no faithful schema model in either direction (it accepts named-invalid values like 'medium' yet rejects valid-canonical values against an invented set); the skill enforces the real schema.

residual_gap: The 72% generation ceiling is not noise. All 5/18 skill failures are the task 'related: DESIGN-NNN' traceability field emitted as empty/absent when a task is generated in isolation with no upstream design context. This is the skill's own documented-vs-validator tension (SKILL requires related for tasks) and would recur for early-in-pipeline /spec task generation. It hits both prompts equally.
honest_findings: The agent's 0/18 is caused jointly by missing required fields (created/updated/related) AND enum drift; missing fields are an independent sufficient cause.
The held-out eval shows the agent's schema 'knowledge' is confabulated (invents plausible enum sets that do not match canonical), which is the root cause of the shipped invalid frontmatter.

One harness before-pass (S3) was earned for the wrong reason (agent rejected on hallucinated blocked_by/blocks/assignee requirements, not the status=ready enum drift), so before_score 0.2 slightly flatters the agent.
The scenario set is REJECT-heavy (4-5 REJECT / 1 ACCEPT); over-rejection is guarded by a single ACCEPT scenario, so '100%' is weak evidence the skill avoids over-rejecting valid specs.
what_evidence_does_not_prove: Does NOT prove the skill emits complete valid frontmatter at generation time (72%, not 100%).
Does NOT prove robustness across models or temperatures (single model, temperature 0; the 3-run flakiness protocol cannot surface variance at temp 0).

Does NOT isolate the validator as the cause: before is a full agent prompt, after is a full SKILL.md; the comparison measures the whole rewrite (persona + schema-read instruction + inline enum table + validator gate).

Does NOT exercise the bundled-validator MECHANISM live: the harness sends no tools, so the skill's 'run validate_spec_frontmatter.py' step is unfollowable in the eval; the live wins come from the inline enum table. The validator's correctness is proven separately by the 20-case contract test.

manual_review.reviewed_by: agent + 4-lens adversarial workflow (methodology, confounds, claims-honesty, reproducibility) + lead synthesis
